ranked_targets.target: MoE backward return skew plus overlap
ranked_targets.read: The raw dX combine kernel is sub-ms; the wall is peer-arrival skew hidden inside combine. Highest structural return is start dX return earlier or in chunks, and reduce owner-rank VJP skew.
This is the VJP imbalance that feeds combine wait. The next kernel return is fused/grouped owner-rank training GEMM and better scheduling, not dispatch-only changes.

The full-vocab loss path is a real multi-second bucket. The easy chunk-count A/B found CE2 faster than the old CE8 registered-combine baseline, while CE4 and CE1 regressed; the follow-up launcher-default validation passed at 3103 tok/GPU/s.

ranked_targets.target: Attention forward/backward and final sparse MLA/LI
ranked_targets.read: Attention is not the largest current guarded-path bucket, but final sparse MLA/LI is still mandatory because the retained systems canary uses guarded fallback/prototype sparse backward rather than the final fused path.
Meaningful but below MoE and loss for expected near-term return.
Not the largest EP8 bucket in the current single-node profile; revisit after MoE/loss changes alter overlap.

non_additivity_caveat: Profiler and sidecar buckets use different synchronization and annotation scopes; use the ranking and A/B throughput direction rather than summing all rows as wall time.
next_actions: Keep CE2 as the retained loss-chunk setting for the exact 8x target shape; CE1 was validated on a clean idle 8-GPU node and regressed to 3002 tok/GPU/s.
Implement the staged AsyncLL dX overlap contract in run_artifacts/mori_asyncll_dx_overlap_contract_20260603.json rather than more manual MORI launch tuple sweeps.
Continue fused/grouped owner-rank VJP work because VJP skew feeds combine wait.
Keep final sparse MLA/LI as mandatory correctness/perf work even though current guarded-path attention is not the top bucket.
